change_comments.message:
Hi Rajesh,

God approach to use modelloader etc to introduce new data like in ncmp-service. 

However this code is all new but seems for a great part duplication of the code in ncmp-service.
Obviously some refactoring should take place here first. I even recommend a separate commit to move the common functionality to CPS (its OK for NCMP to depend on CPS but not the other way!)
I would prefer a paper(wiki) based analysis/proposal first which can be discussed and greed with team before committing anymore code...

this patch doesnt take care of removing the duplicate code from NCMP side??
testware update etc.
NCMP code refactoring to remove duplicate code will be in separate patch
NCMP code cleanup was planned for separate patch. do you want to add NCML changes also with same patch?
marked as WIP as the build is not passing

marking as WIP for now until we do the release.
will review it soon though.
if u can resolve the merge conflict then I can ask ppl to review this.
few NB issues. Otherwise its just code movement from NCMP to CPS side to avoid duplication.
since its just code movement from NCMP to CPS side to avoid code duplication.
if you take out the initial subscription creation its actually almost OK really...

inline_comments.message: Dependency Injection: Can we use JsonObjectMapper bean in this class as a bean?
Exiting application due to failure in onboarding model...
log.info("Data node for dataspace '{}' already exists under '{}'.", dataspace .getName(), REGISTRY_DATANODE_NAME);
log.error("Failed to create data node for dataspace '{}': {}", dataspace .getName(), exception.getMessage());

rename method name as suggested into previous comment

loggingListAppender.stop()
Application started event handles startup exception
a startup exception is thrown during model onboarding
Creating a dataspace delegates to the service
the exception is ignored, and a log message is produced
dataspace service throws a runtime exception
Creating a schema set delegates to the service
Creating a schema set handles already defined exception
Creating a schema set from a non-existing YANG file
attempting to create a schema set from a non-existing fil
Creating an anchor delegates to the service
Creating an anchor handles already defined exception

then: 'the exception is ignored, and a log message is produced'
            noExceptionThrown()
 def logs = loggingListAppender.list.toString()
            assert logs.contains('Creating new anchor failed as anchor already exists')
Creating an anchor handles other exceptions
Creating a top-level data node delegates to the service
Creating a top-level node handles already defined exception

then: 'the exception is ignored, and a log message is produced'
            noExceptionThrown()
            assertLogContains('failed as data node already exists')

// Stop the appender after tests to prevent memory leaks

loggingListAppender.stop()

i think we call it as ModelLoader in NCMP. So I guess we should follow similar stuff atleast for the base interfaces and abstract classes.

CpsModelLoader suggested. and same comments applicable for all the places.
amm.. this is not spring managed so I guess no. we wont be able to do DI here.
i think we agreed that all this Notification Subscription stuff will be part of a different/new dataspace. right ?
?? can we be more descriptive here.
same comment as before w.r.t the dataspace and anchors we are going to use.
dependency injection cannot be done here because ObjectMapper parameter is needed for instantiation. similarly implemented at org.onap.cps.ncmp.init.AbstractModelLoader
Yes, CPS-Admin is that new dataspace
I think Optional is also doing the same. it checks for null and empty list before subscription.
After removing Spy, test cases are failing. this was referred from ncmp test cases. we can discuss about alternative solution

Does not check for an empty list (dataSpaces.isEmpty()), so if dataSpaces is an empty list, forEach would just do nothing.

If we need to handle empty lists , go with this approach.

We should avoid it here as:

1. Unnecessary complexity for a simple null check.
2. Misuse of Optional in control flow rather than return types.
3. Potential performance overhead due to object creation.

Removed Unused Fields: 

The mapYangResourcesToContent field was declared but never used, I suspect if we need it.
assertLogContains('Creating new schema set failed as schema set already exists')
assertLogContains('Creating new anchor failed as anchor already exists')

assertLogContains("Data node for dataspace 'test' already exists under 'dataspaces'")
    }
assertLogContains('failed as data node already exists')

Unified Exception Handling: We can have a new test case/ merged with previous to handle AlreadyDefinedException gracefully, maintaining clarity on how exceptions are logged and handled.:
 
 
 def 'Create node for datastore handles already defined exception gracefully.'() {
        given: 'dataspace is already present'
            mockCpsDataspaceService.getAllDataspaces() >> [new Dataspace('test')]
        and: 'the data service throws an already defined exception'
            mockCpsDataService.saveData(*_) >> { throw AlreadyDefinedException.forDataNodes([], 'some context') }
        when: 'attempt to create datastore'
            objectUnderTest.createInitialSubscription(CPS_DATASPACE_NAME, ANCHOR_NAME)
        then: 'the exception is ignored, and a log message is produced'
            noExceptionThrown()
            assertLogContains('failed as data node already exists')
    }
I think we can discuss on this. after discussion I will make suggested changes.
Added test case 'Creating initial subscription handles already defined exception gracefully'
